description_long: This is a dark blue, single-propeller military airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a bubble canopy cockpit. The wings are slightly bent downward and feature prominent white star insignias, typical of World War II-era U.S. Navy aircraft. The fuselage displays the number '86' in white and has red vent details near the engine cowling. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The propeller is four-bladed and mounted at the nose, while the landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable.
description: A dark blue, single-propeller military airplane with U.S. Navy markings, bubble canopy, and distinctive bent wings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four-bladed propeller, bubble canopy, and the full width of the bent wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the star insignia on the wing and tail, the number '86', and the engine cowling details.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, tail configuration, and the star insignia on the right wing.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the star insignia on the fuselage and wing, the number '86', and the cockpit profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with pronounced anhedral angle and no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a four-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable taildragger configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Mid-fuselage, enclosed bubble canopy, single-seat configuration.
